headline: Agent-native tooling and local-model deployments led the week, with Cursor and Gemma pushing on-device multi-agent orchestration.
body: Local models and open source broke out alongside power iteration, and the clearest concrete signal came from on-device agent infrastructure: Gemma 4 ran 10 independent subagents on a single local machine above 170 tokens per second with orchestrator-to-subagent delegation, and Cursor used its Compile 26 keynote to announce Cursor Mobile, the Origin Git platform, cloud agent updates, and a model trained from scratch, reporting that 95% of usage is now agents. A wave of SEO-focused agent content accumulated around Hermes, Claude Code, NotebookLM, and Agent OS, with NotebookLM repositioned as a full agent capable of writing code and building sources autonomously. A separate cluster of new entrants appeared on the infrastructure side: data backup, immutability, disaster recovery, and ransomware protection, tied to a discussion of cybersecurity ROI.
